advantages: Reveal intrinsic directions and scaling factors of a linear transformation, simplifying analysis of powers A^k and dynamical systems.
Enable diagonalization of diagonalizable matrices, turning matrix functions and exponentials into scalar operations on eigenvalues.
Symmetric eigendecompositions yield orthogonal bases and real spectra—ideal for variance decomposition (PCA) and quadratic forms.
Spectral quantities (trace, determinant, spectral radius) summarize global matrix behavior in a few numbers.
disadvantages: Not every matrix is diagonalizable over the reals; defective matrices need Jordan form, which is harder to interpret.
Characteristic polynomial and exact eigendecomposition are expensive O(n³) and numerically delicate for large n.
Eigenvalues alone can be misleading for non-normal matrices where eigenvectors are far from orthogonal.
Complex eigenvalues appear even for real matrices, complicating geometric intuition in low dimensions.
